candidates.item: Future roadmap features advertised on site: file sending/receiving with virus scanning, email life extension up to 72 hours, custom domain selection, and API integration.
candidates.why_interesting: The virus-scanning on received files is a quirky, specific feature for a throwaway email service — most disposable email providers offer zero attachment handling, so bundling antivirus scanning into a burner inbox is a genuinely unexpected pairing worth reacting to.
candidates.recency_note: Roadmap items — not yet shipped; timing unknown.

candidates.item: It generates free, anonymous, encrypted email addresses that automatically self-destruct after 6 hours. No registration is required.
candidates.why_interesting: The 6-hour hard-expiry window is a concrete, specific design choice — it is a tighter TTL than many competitors (some allow 10 minutes, others indefinite), and pairing it with encryption (not just anonymity) is a slightly more distinctive positioning than a plain throwaway inbox.
candidates.recency_note: Described as current product behaviour at time of research.

excluded_and_why.item: Tempxzy was hiring a TikTok UGC Content Creator (remote, US) as recently as March 2026.
excluded_and_why.reason: Mildly interesting as a signal the company is real and growing, but a UGC creator hire is not distinctive or surprising — many small SaaS/consumer-privacy tools run TikTok growth plays. Does not clear the 'huh, that's neat' bar.

excluded_and_why.item: CRITICAL FLAG: This signup exhibits all hallmarks of a throwaway/bot registration.
excluded_and_why.reason: This is an internal data-quality / trust signal about the signee, not a fact about the company worth complimenting. Not a candidate.

excluded_and_why.item: Tempxzy is listed alongside other disposable email providers in verifymail.io domain analysis pages.
excluded_and_why.reason: Pure classification metadata; says nothing interesting or distinctive about the product.
